8
- **Your merge request, before it exists.** Review your entire Git branch—including uncommitted files—in a local MR-style browser UI before you push.
8
+ **AI wrote a pile of AI slop. Your name is still on the MR.**
9
9
 
10
- local-mr brings committed, staged, unstaged, and untracked changes together on one review page. Move between push snapshots, commits, ranges, and a virtual worktree version without modifying the real Git index.
10
+ **Virtual commits are virtual. Your accountability is not.**
11
11
 
12
- **No commit required · Loopback only · Real Git index untouched**
12
+ **Virtual Commit** does not turn it into good code. It only rearranges the committed Diff into an order a human can review: documentation and contracts first, then interfaces, critical paths, risky behavior, and tests—with generated noise safely left for last.
13
+
14
+ Real commits stay untouched. Not a line of code disappears. You still own the change. At least now you can see what you own.
15
+
16
+ ## Reorder the review, not the history
17
+
18
+ Real commits record how the code was written. That is not always the order in which the code makes sense. When an agent changes many files in one pass, Virtual Commit adds a second, review-only route through exactly the same change.
19
+
20
+ ```text
21
+ real history: C1 ───────────── C2 ───────────── C3
22
+ freeze the committed Diff
23
+ │
24
+ review route: V1 docs/contracts → V2 interfaces → V3 risky behavior → V4 tests
25
+
26
+ complete Real range = complete Virtual range
27
+ ```
28
+
29
+ | | Real commits | Virtual commits |
30
+ | --- | --- | --- |
31
+ | Purpose | Record how the branch actually changed | Make the same change easier to review |
32
+ | Order | Git first-parent order | Agent-planned dependency and risk order |
33
+ | Effect on Git | Read-only | Read-only; creates no Git commit or object |
34
+ | Complete range | Frozen committed Diff | The exact same frozen committed Diff |
35
+
36
+ The agent proposes the route; local-mr does not blindly trust it. The engine freezes an exact base-to-commit source, divides it into stable change blocks, rejects missing or duplicate blocks, materializes every cumulative Virtual state in isolation, and verifies that the final tree matches the frozen real tree. Intermediate Virtual states may be incomplete because they are reading steps, not replacement history.
37
+
38
+ Install the bundled Codex Skill once, then choose the review depth and reading order you want:
39
+
40
+ ```bash
41
+ local-mr virtual-commit install-skill codex
42
+ ```
43
+
44
+ ```text
45
+ Use local-mr virtual commits to organize the committed changes against
46
+ origin/main. Use Deep review: put documentation and contracts first, then
47
+ dependencies, high-risk behavior, integrations, and tests. Keep generated
48
+ files last and open the review when it is ready.
49
+ ```
50
+
51
+ The browser opens the generated route in the normal Diff workspace with **Real / Virtual** switching, Single commit and Commit range modes, previous/next navigation, risk and review-focus guidance, immutable revisions, and reviewed progress. See [Virtual Commit reference](#virtual-commit-reference) for the complete workflow and [the dedicated guide](docs/virtual-commits.md) for the manifest and JSON CLI.
13
52
 
14

15

18
57
  - **Keep the review local.** The server listens only on the loopback interface behind a random path token, and worktree snapshots use a temporary index.

+ ## Virtual Commit reference
121
+
122
+ Real commit history records how a change was built; it is not always the best order in which to review it. A large AI-produced commit may mix foundations, risky behavior, integrations, tests, and generated files. Virtual commits let an agent reorganize that same change into a dependency-aware reading route without rebasing, cherry-picking, or creating any Git object in the repository.
123
+
124
+ A virtual commit is a named set of frozen change blocks with an intent, one to three anchored review-focus notes, and a risk assessment. It exists only inside local-mr:
125
+
126
+ ```text
127
+ merge base B ───────────── selected real commit H
128
+ frozen diff D
129
+ │ split into stable blocks
130
+ ▼
131
+ V1 → V2 → … → Vn
132
+
133
+ every block in D appears exactly once
134
+ state after Vn equals the frozen state at H
135
+ ```
136
+
137
+ This makes Virtual commits useful for reviewing large or poorly partitioned changes, teaching the architecture of a branch, or putting high-risk behavior before mechanical edits. They are reading steps, not replacement history: an intermediate Virtual state is allowed to be incomplete or uncompilable, and nothing is ever applied back to Git.
138
+
139
+ ### How it works
140
+
141
+ 1. `snapshot` freezes exactly one committed comparison from the target merge base to a selected real commit. Worktree endpoints are rejected, so staged, unstaged, untracked, and other uncommitted changes cannot enter the source.
142
+ 2. Ordinary text changes are divided into stable contiguous blocks. Renames, binary files, submodules, mode changes, and other unsafe-to-split changes remain whole file-level blocks.
143
+ 3. The agent reads the file/block catalog lazily and writes a manifest that assigns every block to one ordered Virtual commit.
144
+ 4. local-mr rejects unknown, missing, or duplicate blocks and invalid review anchors. It then materializes every cumulative state in an isolated temporary Git repository and verifies that the final tree equals the frozen target tree.
145
+ 5. The accepted plan is stored as an immutable revision and opened in the same Diff workspace as Real review. A later edit appends a revision instead of overwriting history; each revision retains the exact frozen branch commit used to generate it.
146
+
147
+ The source records exact base, head, and target SHAs, a SHA-256 of the canonical patch, and content-addressed copies of affected files. Real and Virtual use the same binary/full-index diff format, so their **complete ranges have the same patch and rendered Diff**. Arbitrary partial ranges need not match: Real follows first-parent Git history, while Virtual intentionally follows a different reading order.
148
+
149
+ The workflow is a read-only projection. It never changes the worktree, real index, object database, refs, commits, Git configuration, remotes, or a remote MR.
150
+
151
+ ### Use it with Codex
152
+
153
+ Install the bundled official Skill once:
154
+
155
+ ```bash
156
+ local-mr virtual-commit install-skill codex
157
+ ```
158
+
159
+ Use `--force` only when intentionally updating an existing installed copy. Then start Codex in the repository and ask for a review depth and reading order, for example:
160
+
161
+ ```text
162
+ Use local-mr virtual commits to organize the committed changes against
163
+ origin/main. Use Deep review with a dependency-aware, core/risk-first reading
164
+ order and open the review when it is ready.
165
+ ```
166
+
167
+ Review depth and reading order are independent. **Overview** usually creates 5–8 broad, mostly whole-file chapters; **Deep review** usually creates 10–20 narrower steps and may place blocks from one large file in different Virtual Commits. If depth is omitted, the Skill snapshots the comparison, asks once with its actual size, and recommends Deep review for large or AI-produced changes. If no reading order is supplied, it uses dependency-aware core/risk-first. It inspects only the frozen files and blocks needed for each grouping decision, submits the manifest, fixes any structured validation errors against the same source, and opens the tokenized loopback review in the browser without relaying its URL.
168
+
169
+ The human chooses the review depth; the model proposes the reading plan. local-mr independently enforces source immutability, block conservation, final-tree equality, persistence, and Git safety.
170
+
171
+ ### Review Real and Virtual side by side
172
+
173
+ The source switch at the top is part of every review, so bare `local-mr` and Virtual Review links always open the same workspace. The bare command is the stable, live page for one repository, branch, and target; it automatically discovers a saved Virtual Review for that identity. If the branch moves afterward, the plan does not disappear: the switch reads **Virtual · stale** and, after you enter it, a persistent banner names both the frozen commit and current `HEAD` while warning that later commits are not included. With no saved match, the Virtual side stays visible and explicitly unavailable instead of making the page change shape. Both sources share the changed-file tree, lazy loading, side-by-side and line-by-line layouts, syntax highlighting, read markers, omitted-context expansion, Markdown/Mermaid preview, mobile drawer, and exactly two comparison modes:
174
+
175
+ - **Single commit** jumps to any commit in the current source's order. For `Vn`, it compares the cumulative state after `Vn-1` with the state after `Vn`.
176
+ - **Commit range** compares an inclusive contiguous interval. For `V3…V6`, the left side is the state after `V2` and the right side is the state after `V6`. A one-commit range is valid.
177
+
178
+ Real opens on the complete committed **Commit range**, follows first-parent order, and may expose the worktree as an explicit final checkpoint. A Real range beginning at the first review commit is anchored to the merge base, preventing target-only changes from reappearing after the target was merged into the feature branch. Virtual opens in **Single commit** at `V1`, then follows `V1`, `V2`, … order with previous/next navigation, revision selection, and revision-local reviewed progress. The revision selector identifies each plan's frozen branch commit by SHA, subject, and branch; revisions in one review may intentionally point to different commits. The shared context panel shows the real Git subject/body or the Virtual intent, review focus, risk, and anchored guidance.
179
+
180
+ When a stale plan is entered from the live Real page, its Real button returns to that same live page; a historical Virtual link opened directly still uses the frozen Real comparison for exact source-to-source verification. Switching to frozen Real and back preserves the exact Virtual revision, mode, endpoints, and focused file. Frozen Real opens with the snapshot's base, head, and target SHAs, so later repository drift or stale target configuration cannot silently change the file set. There is no Pushes mode; legacy `mode=push` URLs are accepted only as aliases and normalized to Single commit or Commit range.
181
+
182
+ ### Direct JSON CLI
183
+
184
+ The bundled Skill uses a versioned, automation-friendly interface that is also available directly:
185
+
186
+ ```text
187
+ local-mr virtual-commit snapshot [--target REF] [--mode MODE --from VALUE --to VALUE]
188
+ local-mr virtual-commit show SOURCE_ID [--file PATH | --block ID | --full]
189
+ local-mr virtual-commit create SOURCE_ID [--manifest FILE] [--review ID] [--expected-revision N] [--no-open]
190
+ local-mr virtual-commit open REVIEW_ID [--revision N] [--no-open]
191
+ local-mr virtual-commit list
192
+ local-mr virtual-commit delete REVIEW_ID [--revision N]
193
+ local-mr virtual-commit prune
194
+ ```
195
+
196
+ The common manual lifecycle is:
197
+
198
+ ```bash
199
+ local-mr virtual-commit snapshot --target origin/main
200
+ local-mr virtual-commit show SOURCE_ID --file src/router.ts
201
+ local-mr virtual-commit create SOURCE_ID --manifest manifest.json
202
+ local-mr virtual-commit list
203
+ local-mr virtual-commit open REVIEW_ID --revision 2
204
+ local-mr virtual-commit delete REVIEW_ID
205
+ local-mr virtual-commit prune
206
+ ```
207
+
208
+ `show` can return one file, one block, or the full patch. `create` reads stdin when `--manifest` is omitted; `--review REVIEW_ID` appends an immutable revision, and `--expected-revision N` prevents racing updates. `list` reports the frozen branch commit for every revision. `create` and `open` launch the browser unless `--no-open` is supplied. Successful commands emit one JSON object on stdout; failures emit structured JSON on stderr.
209
+
210
+ Snapshots are self-contained and remain reviewable if the repository moves or disappears. They are kept outside the repository under `$XDG_STATE_HOME/local-mr/virtual-reviews`, or `~/.local/state/local-mr/virtual-reviews` when `XDG_STATE_HOME` is unset. They contain private source code and persist until explicitly managed, so protect that directory and use `delete` followed by `prune` to remove reviews and their now-unreferenced snapshots/blobs. Virtual-commit commands neither contact nor modify a remote MR, nor change the worktree, index, refs, commits, or repository object database.
211
+
212
+ See the [complete Virtual Commit guide](docs/virtual-commits.md) for the manifest schema, explicit source selection, revision workflow, validation errors, storage model, and cleanup instructions.
213
+
81
214
  ## Privacy and security
82
215
 
83
216
  local-mr serves each review on `127.0.0.1` behind a random path token and never writes to the repository's real Git index. The page can still contain private source code, so do not share review URLs, runtime logs, or unsanitized screenshots. Report vulnerabilities privately as described in [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md).
84
217
 
218
+ CI scans the complete Git history with a pinned Gitleaks image. Contributors who already have Gitleaks installed can optionally enable the repository's staged pre-commit hook. See the [contributing guide](CONTRIBUTING.md#sensitive-data) for details.
219
+

heading: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)
title: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)

focusedCommit.label: Human review index
focusedCommit.title: Review the MR in human order
focusedCommit.description: Deep review · docs-first, then dependency and risk order. Fourteen focused steps keep each reviewer question narrow without relying on source line numbers.
reviewFocus: V1 · Read the contract before the implementation
V2 · Map the CLI and agent contract
V3 · Freeze the source and define stable change blocks
V4 · Reconstruct the exact target tree
V6 · Discover the right saved review
V7 · Pin runtime identity and source handoff
V8 · Serve Virtual comparisons and context
V9 · Integrate Virtual history into the shared server
V10 · Navigate the shared review workspace

commits.subject: Read the contract before the implementation
commits.body: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.description: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.intent: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Extract the source-freezing, block-conservation, and final-tree guarantees before reading their implementation.
Check that the concise README workflow agrees with the detailed guide.

risk.reason: The documentation defines the reviewer's mental model and must not promise guarantees the code does not enforce.

commits.subject: Map the CLI and agent contract
commits.body: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.description: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.intent: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Trace manifest input from the CLI boundary through schema and block-assignment validation.
Confirm the bundled Skill describes the same protocol and safety boundary enforced by the CLI.

risk.reason: This contract decides which frozen source an agent may reorganize and which invalid plans are rejected.

commits.subject: Freeze the source and define stable change blocks
commits.body: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.description: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.intent: Verify merge-base selection and the deterministic block model before any change is reordered into a Virtual Commit.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check that target-only history cannot leak into the frozen review range.
Verify stable identities for text, binary, rename, delete, and submodule changes.

risk.reason: Every later conservation guarantee depends on freezing the right Diff and naming each block exactly once.

commits.subject: Reconstruct the exact target tree
commits.body: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.description: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.intent: Follow cumulative block application from the frozen base through every Virtual state to the real feature commit tree.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Verify missing, duplicate, unknown, or malformed block assignments fail closed.
Confirm final-tree equality is checked after materialization rather than inferred from block counts.

risk.reason: A flaw here could silently omit or alter code while presenting a plausible review route.

commits.subject: Protect immutable review history
commits.body: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.description: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.intent: Audit revision storage, locking, atomic writes, permissions, progress, and cleanup.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check concurrent revision creation and recovery from interrupted writes.
Confirm permissions and cleanup match a private-source-code storage boundary.

risk.reason: Mutable or weakly protected snapshots would undermine reproducibility and source confidentiality.

commits.subject: Discover the right saved review
commits.body: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.description: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.intent: Verify that the default command attaches the newest compatible revision to the current repository comparison.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check repository, target, base, and head matching before a saved revision is attached.
Confirm stale revisions remain visible without being mistaken for the current source.

risk.reason: Choosing the wrong saved review can put valid Virtual Commit labels over unrelated source code.

commits.subject: Pin runtime identity and source handoff
commits.body: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.description: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.intent: Follow a stored revision into its runtime while keeping repository, source, and revision identity immutable.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Confirm opening a stored review cannot silently fall back to live HEAD or another repository.
Check stale-source reporting without mutating the frozen source.

risk.reason: An identity mix-up could show valid code from the wrong source while retaining a trusted review URL.

commits.subject: Serve Virtual comparisons and context
commits.body: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.description: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.intent: Audit selection parsing, tree-to-tree comparison, context expansion, previews, and Real or Virtual navigation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Verify every selection resolves to the intended before and after Virtual states.
Check that context, previews, IDs, paths, and revisions remain scoped to the frozen source.

risk.reason: This boundary turns stored state into reviewer-visible code and handles untrusted URL input.

commits.subject: Integrate Virtual history into the shared server
commits.body: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.description: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.intent: Verify discovery, source attachment, routes, and version metadata remain consistent in the normal Local MR server.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Trace how the default Local MR page discovers and attaches a compatible Virtual review.
Check route and cache identity when switching between Real and Virtual sources.

risk.reason: A bad handoff can make the normal review page display metadata or Diff content from another comparison.

commits.subject: Navigate the shared review workspace
commits.body: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.description: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.intent: Read the browser state machine for modes, ranges, revisions, Real and Virtual switching, context, previews, and restored selections.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Follow a selection through URL state, data loading, source switching, and history restoration.
Check that syntax, context, previews, and controls never imply a different comparison from the Diff on screen.

risk.reason: A misleading workspace can make reviewers approve the wrong comparison even when server data is correct.

commits.subject: Prove the core invariants with adversarial tests
commits.body: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.description: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.intent: Match source freezing, block conservation, manifest validation, and final-tree equality to executable failure cases.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Look for target merges, drift, missing blocks, duplicate blocks, binary data, renames, and submodules.
Confirm negative tests prove fail-closed behavior rather than only friendly error text.

risk.reason: These tests are the executable evidence that reordering changes presentation rather than code.

commits.subject: Stress persistence, discovery, and installation
commits.body: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.description: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.intent: Inspect regressions for concurrent revisions, saved-review matching, frozen runtime identity, attachment, and package installation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check concurrent writers, immutable revisions, stale repositories, and compatible review discovery.
Verify the installed package includes the CLI and bundled Skill needed by the documented workflow.

risk.reason: Cross-process and installed-package failures often escape core unit tests.

commits.subject: Exercise the complete review experience
commits.body: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.description: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.intent: Finish authored behavior with the remaining integration and browser regressions for rendering, context, caching, previews, and read state.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Confirm browser tests operate controls and inspect the resulting Diff rather than only static markup.
Check cache, context, Markdown, read-state, and renderer regressions around the shared workspace.

risk.reason: This is where otherwise-correct subsystems combine into the comparison a human actually approves.
